opp.ais_why:
• Carmeuse is early: next step is a 'Deeper discovery call setup for first week of August,' with no discovery notes yet captured.
• Merlin and Merlin Intake are scoped, indicating AI-enabled modules are in the frame, but no agentic discussion or sentiment is recorded.
• Evidence is limited to product tags and a scheduling step, so AI appetite cannot be placed above exploratory.

sources: Salesforce Next_Step__c (2026-06-29): 'Got in touch with Blake Miller again, he is currently in internal discussions with his team, setup a deeper discovery for first week of August. Need to setup a deeper discovery call in a week, the prospect will get back to us about their initiative.'
No Avoma calls discovered (opp + account pull, 2025-01-01 to 2026-06-30).

Salesforce custom fields query (Q3) returned no values for Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c.
metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI case on record. Salesforce fields Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, and Compelling_Event__c returned no values (attempted read, fields likely unpopulated or not present in this org). No Avoma calls exist to extract buyer-stated pain or savings targets. Next step references 'internal discussions with his team' and 'their initiative,' implying Blake Miller is building an internal case, but we have not yet heard the business driver, cost of inaction, or success metrics in his words. The deeper discovery call scheduled for early August 2026 is the forcing function to extract: what operational or financial pain is driving this S2P evaluation, what does success look like in 12 months, and what happens if they do nothing. Until that call, the deal rests on assumption, not evidence.

sources: Salesforce OpportunityContactRole query: only Blake Miller returned (no title, no role, no champion flag).

Salesforce Next_Step__c: 'Got in touch with Blake Miller again, he is currently in internal discussions with his team' (shows Blake is engaged and doing internal work, but does not prove advocacy or access).
No Avoma calls to assess Blake's advocacy, access, or coaching behavior.
Salesforce Q3 custom fields (X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Decision_Maker_Name_Title__c, Decision_Maker_Identified__c) returned no values.
champion.narrative: No champion identified. Blake Miller is the only contact on record; his title, seniority, and role in the buying process are unknown from the relevant stakeholder calls exist to assess his advocacy, access to the Economic Buyer or Decision Maker, or willingness to coach Zycus on internal politics and competitive positioning. The next step ('he is currently in internal discussions with his team') suggests Blake is building an internal case, which is a positive signal (he is doing work on our behalf), but we do not know whether he has the credibility, access, or urgency to drive this deal forward, or whether he is a low-level influencer gathering information for someone else. A true champion would introduce Zycus to the EB, share internal decision criteria or timelines, push back on competitors in our favor, and actively drive the evaluation forward. The August discovery call must assess Blake's strength: does he have a seat at the decision table, does he have access to the budget holder, is he personally invested in solving the pain (and why), and can he introduce us to other stakeholders (the EB, DM, a business-unit sponsor, or an IT/InfoSec gatekeeper). If Blake is weak or politically isolated, this deal will stall. If he is strong, we must develop him into an active champion by giving him the ammunition (ROI story, references, competitive wedge, Merlin AI differentiation) to sell Zycus internally.

sources: Salesforce Q3 custom fields (Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Why_not_Zycus__c, Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c) returned no values.
No Avoma calls.
Salesforce Next_Step__c: 'he is currently in internal discussions with his team, the prospect will get back to us about their initiative' (competitive landscape unknown).
competition.narrative: No competition mapped. Salesforce fields Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, and Why_not_Zycus__c returned no values. No Avoma calls exist to reveal whether Carmeuse is benchmarking Zycus against SAP Ariba, Coupa, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua, or another S2P incumbent, or whether this is a sole-source evaluation (Zycus is the only vendor being considered). The next step implies Carmeuse is 'in internal discussions' about 'their initiative,' suggesting they may still be defining requirements and have not yet invited competitors to the table, or they are running a quiet competitive eval and have not disclosed it. The August discovery call must extract: are other vendors being considered (and who), what is Carmeuse's current state (manual processes, an incumbent S2P platform, or a patchwork of point solutions), and what would make them choose Zycus over an alternative. For a $110K S2P suite with Merlin AI, the competitive wedge is likely: (1) Merlin's agentic negotiation and intake automation (no other S2P vendor has this depth in generative AI for supplier negotiation); (2) unified S2P platform vs stitched-together modules (Coupa and Ariba require integration tax); (3) speed to value and ease of use (Zycus is faster to deploy than SAP/Oracle). But without knowing who we are competing against or what Carmeuse values most, we cannot activate the wedge.

sources: Salesforce Next_Step__c: 'he is currently in internal discussions with his team, the prospect will get back to us about their initiative' (pain implied but not stated).
Salesforce Q3 custom fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, Compelling_Event__c) returned no values.
No Avoma calls.
Product scope (iContract, iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eInvoicing, Merlin, Merlin Intake) implies broad pain across S2P, but no evidence of what is most urgent.
identify_pain.narrative: No pain identified or quantified. Salesforce fields Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, and Compelling_Event__c returned no values. No Avoma calls exist to capture what is broken today in Carmeuse's procurement/contracting/spend management (e.g., manual contract redlining, supplier fragmentation, maverick spend, invoice reconciliation errors, lack of spend visibility, or inability to negotiate supplier terms at scale). The next step implies Blake is 'in internal discussions with his team' about 'their initiative,' suggesting pain exists but has not been articulated to Zycus yet. The product scope (iContract, iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eInvoicing, Merlin Intake) implies pain across contracts, sourcing, spend analytics, supplier management, invoicing, and intake/negotiation automation, but we do not know which is the primary driver or the cost of inaction. The August discovery call must extract: what specific operational or financial pain is driving this evaluation, who owns that pain (procurement, finance, legal, or a business unit), what is the cost of not solving it (delayed projects, compliance risk, lost savings, manual FTE burn), and what success looks like in 12 months. Without this, the deal is speculative.

sources: Salesforce Q2/Q3 custom fields (Current_Contract_Expiration__c) returned no value.
No Avoma calls.
No Salesforce tasks or events referencing MSA, SOW, legal, InfoSec, or SI/partner.
paper_process.narrative: No paper process or contracting mechanics mapped. Salesforce field Current_Contract_Expiration__c returned no value, so it is unknown whether Carmeuse is replacing an incumbent vendor under a contract that expires on a forcing-function date, or building a procurement platform for the first time. No MSA, SOW, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec requirements, or SI/partner involvement has been discussed. No Avoma calls exist to reveal whether Carmeuse has standard procurement terms, a preferred SI (e.g., Deloitte, Accenture, or a regional partner), or a legal/InfoSec gate that could delay contracting. The August discovery call should surface: is there an existing contract that expires (and when), does Carmeuse require an MSA review or InfoSec questionnaire before they can proceed to pricing, and will implementation be led by Zycus, a partner, or Carmeuse's internal team. For a $110K S2P suite buy with Merlin AI, InfoSec and data-privacy review is likely; knowing the timeline for that review is critical to defending the May 2027 close date.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified. For a $110K S2P platform buy spanning iContract, iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eInvoicing, and Merlin AI, budget authority likely sits with a VP Procurement, CFO, or CPO, not a single procurement manager. Blake Miller is the only contact on record; his title and seniority are unknown from Salesforce, and no calls exist to reveal his authority or who he reports to. Salesforce fields X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c, and Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c returned no values. The next step implies Blake is 'in internal discussions with his team,' suggesting he is building a case upward, not approving it himself. The August discovery call must map the approval chain: who controls the budget, who signs the contract, and whether Blake has direct access to that person or needs to earn it. Until the EB is named and engaged, this deal cannot progress past discovery.

sources: Salesforce Next_Step__c: 'he is currently in internal discussions with his team, the prospect will get back to us about their initiative' (process still being defined).
Salesforce Q3 custom fields (What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Multiple_approvals__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c) returned no values.

decision_process.narrative: No decision process mapped. Salesforce fields What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Multiple_approvals__c, and Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c returned no values. No Avoma calls exist to reveal whether Carmeuse is running an RFI/RFP, how many vendors are being considered, what the evaluation timeline looks like, or who participates at each stage. The next step implies Blake is 'in internal discussions' and will 'get back to us about their initiative,' suggesting the process is still being defined internally and we are not yet formally invited into a structured eval. The August discovery call must extract: is this a sole-source evaluation or a competitive RFP, what is the timeline from discovery to vendor selection to contract signature, who are the key decision-makers and influencers at each gate, and what milestones (demo, pilot, reference calls, workshop, pricing, legal review) will Carmeuse require before they can commit. Without this, we cannot forecast progression or spot process risks.

sources: Salesforce Q3 custom fields (Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c, Shoe_Fit_Criteria_Met__c) returned no values.
No Avoma calls.
Product scope from Salesforce Products__c: iContract, iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eInvoicing, Merlin, Merlin Intake (authoritative, but criteria for selection unknown).
decision_criteria.narrative: No decision criteria documented. Salesforce fields Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c, and Shoe_Fit_Criteria_Met__c returned no values. No RFP, RFI, or BRD has been shared. No Avoma calls exist to capture what Carmeuse will evaluate vendors against (ease of use, integration with their ERP, AI/Merlin adoption readiness, supplier network coverage, compliance requirements, or total cost of ownership). The product scope (iContract through Merlin Intake) is broad, implying procurement, contracts, spend analytics, supplier management, invoicing, and AI-driven intake/negotiation are all in play, but we do not know which capabilities are must-haves vs nice-to-haves, or what would disqualify a vendor. The August discovery call must extract: what are the top 3-5 criteria Carmeuse will use to compare vendors, what is the relative weight of each, and how does Merlin AI fit their readiness and appetite for agentic procurement. Without this, we are flying blind into any competitive eval.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

No Avoma calls exist to assess whether Carmeuse is 'AI Hungry' (actively seeking agentic procurement AI, sees Merlin as a competitive advantage), 'AI Curious' (interested but cautious, needs proof of ROI and change-management support), or 'AI Resistant' (prefers traditional workflows, sees AI as risky or immature). The August discovery call must extract Carmeuse's AI posture and position Merlin accordingly.

$110K for 7-module S2P suite (iContract, iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eInvoicing, Merlin, Merlin Intake) suggests either phased roll-out or small user base. champion_strength.source: Salesforce OpportunityContactRole (Blake Miller only contact, no title/role); Salesforce Next_Step__c 2026-06-29 ('he is currently in internal discussions with his team'); no Avoma calls to assess advocacy, access, or coaching behavior
champion_strength.summary: No champion identified. Blake Miller is the only contact, but his title, seniority, and role in the buying process are unknown. Next step indicates he is 'in internal discussions with his team' about 'their initiative,' which is a positive signal (he is doing internal work on our behalf), but we do not know whether he has the credibility, access, or urgency to drive this deal forward, or whether he is a low-level influencer gathering information for someone else. A true champion would introduce Zycus to the Economic Buyer, share internal decision criteria or competitive intel, and actively advocate for us. The August discovery call must assess Blake's strength: does he have a seat at the decision table, does he have access to the budget holder, is he personally invested in solving the pain (and why), and can he introduce us to other stakeholders. If Blake is weak, this deal will stall without multi-threading and finding a stronger sponsor.

items.commitment: Conduct deeper discovery call in first week of August 2026 to extract MEDDPICC depth (metrics, pain, EB, criteria, process, champion strength), multi-thread (map at least 2-3 additional stakeholders including EB/DM), map competition and current state, assess Merlin AI appetite, and clarify deployment model and pricing realism.
Blake Miller to complete internal discussions with his team and confirm availability/agenda for deeper discovery call in first week of August 2026.

items.action: Execute deeper discovery call in first week of August 2026 (by 2026-08-07) with Blake Miller and ideally at least one additional Carmeuse stakeholder (EB, procurement director, or business-unit sponsor). Extract MEDDPICC foundation: quantified pain and business case (what is broken, cost of inaction, ROI target), decision criteria and must-haves (evaluation scorecard, AI readiness), decision process and timeline (sole-source vs RFP, milestones to contract signature), Economic Buyer and approval chain (who controls budget, who signs), champion strength (assess Blake's access and advocacy, or identify a stronger sponsor), competition and current state (which vendors are being considered, what Carmeuse uses today), and Merlin AI appetite (agentic negotiation and intake automation as differentiator or distraction). If this call does not deliver substantive answers to at least 5 of 7 MEDDPICC elements, recommend qualify-out rather than chase a speculative deal for 10 months.
items.trigger: Salesforce Next_Step__c 2026-06-29: 'Got in touch with Blake Miller again, he is currently in internal discussions with his team, setup a deeper discovery for first week of August. Need to setup a deeper discovery call in a week, the prospect will get back to us about their initiative.' Zero buyer calls completed to date; this is the first real two-way engagement and the forcing function to build MEDDPICC foundation or walk away.
items.expected_effect: Convert speculative lead to qualified opportunity with confirmed pain, EB alignment, and eval process, or qualify out and free up capacity. Moves deal from Qualified to the relevant stakeholder if successful, or to the relevant stakeholder if MEDDPICC gaps cannot be closed.

items.action: Multi-thread immediately during or after August discovery call. Request that Blake Miller introduce Zycus to at least two additional stakeholders: (1) the Economic Buyer or budget holder (VP Procurement, CFO, CPO), and (2) one other influencer (procurement director, IT/InfoSec lead for Merlin AI data integration, finance for ROI case, or business-unit sponsor for pain ownership). If Blake resists or cannot make introductions, assess whether he has the political capital to drive this deal, and if not, escalate to identify a stronger champion or executive sponsor via the deal owner's manager or a partner channel.
items.trigger: Salesforce OpportunityContactRole query returned only Blake Miller (no title, no role, no other stakeholders). Single-threaded deal is fragile; if Blake deprioritizes or leaves, deal collapses. Next step (2026-06-29) implies Blake is 'in internal discussions with his team,' so other stakeholders exist but Zycus has not met them yet.
items.expected_effect: De-risk single-threading, map EB and approval chain, and build redundancy so deal does not collapse if Blake exits or loses priority. Enables progression to the relevant stakeholder or Shortlisted stage.

items.action: Position Merlin AI (agentic negotiation and intake automation) as the differentiated wedge against likely S2P competitors (SAP Ariba, Coupa, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua) IF August discovery confirms Carmeuse has AI appetite and agentic-procurement readiness. If AI appetite is low or change-management concerns emerge, reposition Merlin as optional/Phase 2 and lead with unified S2P platform simplicity, ease of use, and speed to value. Prepare 2-3 customer references (CPG or industrial manufacturing preferred, matching Carmeuse's sector) who adopted Merlin for supplier negotiation and can speak to ROI and adoption curve. Share these references during or immediately after August discovery to build confidence.
items.trigger: Salesforce Products__c includes Merlin and Merlin Intake (authoritative), but AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) are unpopulated and no Avoma calls exist to assess whether Merlin story is landing or whether Carmeuse sees AI as differentiator vs distraction. For $110K S2P buy, Merlin is likely the wedge against traditional platforms, but must be tailored to buyer's AI maturity.
items.expected_effect: Activate Merlin as competitive wedge if AI appetite is confirmed, or de-risk by repositioning as Phase 2 if appetite is low. Builds buyer confidence via references and differentiation vs Ariba/Coupa. Supports progression to Shortlisted or Vendor Selected.

items.action: Map competition and current-state stack during August discovery. Extract: (1) which other S2P vendors Carmeuse is evaluating (SAP Ariba, Coupa, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua, or sole-source Zycus only), (2) what Carmeuse uses today (manual processes, an incumbent S2P platform under contract that expires on a forcing-function date, or a patchwork of point solutions), and (3) what would make them choose Zycus over an alternative (decision criteria: ease of use, speed to value, Merlin AI, supplier network, pricing, or something else). Use this intel to activate the competitive wedge (Merlin agentic AI, unified platform vs modules, faster deployment than SAP/Oracle) and position Zycus as the best-fit choice on their top 3 criteria.
items.trigger: Salesforce Q3 custom fields (Competitors__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c) returned no values. No Avoma calls exist to reveal competitive landscape. Salesforce Next_Step__c (2026-06-29) implies Carmeuse is 'in internal discussions' but has not disclosed whether other vendors are in play.
items.expected_effect: Identify competitive threats early and activate differentiation wedge before formal RFP stage. Positions Zycus to win on criteria that favor us (Merlin AI, unified platform, ease of use) vs competitors' weaknesses (Ariba/Coupa complexity, lack of agentic AI).

items.action: Clarify deployment model, user count, and pricing realism during August discovery. $110K for 7-module S2P suite (iContract, iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eInvoicing, Merlin, Merlin Intake) suggests either a phased roll-out (start with 2-3 modules, expand later) or a small user base (pilot or single business unit). Extract: (1) how many users and business units will be in scope for initial deployment, (2) whether this is a pilot with expansion potential or a full enterprise roll-out, and (3) whether the $110K amount is a realistic budget that Carmeuse has confirmed or a placeholder. If pricing is misaligned with scope, surface the gap early (before demo or proposal stage) to avoid sticker shock or deal collapse later. If this is a pilot, map the expansion path and criteria for Phase 2 buy.
items.trigger: Salesforce Amount ($110K, authoritative) and Products__c (7 modules, authoritative) suggest potential misalignment: broad scope for relatively low ACV. No Avoma calls or Salesforce fields clarify deployment model or user count.
items.expected_effect: De-risk pricing misalignment and scope-creep before demo or proposal stage. Aligns buyer expectations with realistic pricing and deployment timeline. If this is a pilot, secures commitment to Phase 2 expansion path and prevents deal from stalling at small initial buy.

Zero Avoma calls discovered (opp + account pull, 2025-01-01 to 2026-06-30). No buyer voice captured yet.

confidence_signals.summary: Low engagement density. Zero buyer calls completed (no Avoma transcripts from opp-id or account-id pull). Only signal is rep-entered next step from 2026-06-29 indicating rep reached out to Blake Miller, Blake is in internal discussions, and a deeper discovery call is being scheduled for early August 2026. Last Salesforce activity date is future-dated (2026-08-06, likely a scheduled task placeholder; today is 2026-06-30), so no completed two-way engagement since deal creation 2026-05-26. Deal is LIVE (per engagement pulse) due to recent rep activity, but buyer has not yet fully engaged. Discovery call in early August is the first real test of buyer commitment and urgency.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-05-26, qualified same day (assumed), 35 days in Qualified stage as of 2026-06-30. Close date 2027-05-01 is 305 days out. At current pace (35 days, still in Qualified with no formal eval or shortlist milestone reached), forward projection suggests 10+ months to close, which aligns with the May 2027 target for a $110K S2P suite buy in a large enterprise. No forward slip detected (deal is new). Qualified-to-Close velocity cannot yet be assessed (no progression beyond Qualified). Required pace: move to Formal Evaluation within 60-90 days, Shortlisted by Q4 2026, Vendor Selected by Q1 2027, close Q2 2027. Currently on schedule by calendar, but MEDDPICC and engagement depth are critically thin.
evidence: Last Salesforce activity 2026-08-06 (future-dated, likely a scheduled task or placeholder; today is 2026-06-30).

Next step from Salesforce: 'Got in touch with Blake Miller again, he is currently in internal discussions with his team, setup a deeper discovery for first week of August. Need to setup a deeper discovery call in a week, the prospect will get back to us about their initiative.' This is rep-initiated outreach awaiting buyer confirmation, not completed two-way engagement.
Zero Avoma calls discovered via opp-id or account-id pull (Carmeuse account, carmeuse.com domain match). No transcripts, no buyer voices, no MEDDPICC evidence from calls.
Salesforce custom fields (Q2/Q3 queries) returned no MEDDPICC depth, no competition, no pain quantification, no decision process, no champion, no EB/DM mapped.
Contact roles: only Blake Miller on record (title, role, and engagement level unknown from Salesforce; no other stakeholders).

Products in scope: iContract, iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eInvoicing, Merlin, Merlin Intake (full S2P suite plus Merlin AI). This is a broad, high-value footprint for $110K, suggesting either a phased roll-out or a small user base.
Pipeline forecast is defensible at this stage: deal is 305 days out, early Qualified, discovery not yet completed. Premature to move to Best Case without EB, champion, or confirmed eval process.
north_star_verdict.headline: Single-threaded early-stage deal 305 days out with zero MEDDPICC foundation and no buyer calls completed; discovery call scheduled for early August is make-or-break to build depth or qualify out.

flags: Single-threaded: Only Blake Miller named; no multi-threading evidence (Contact Roles empty, no other stakeholders in Next Step or calls)
No Economic Buyer identified: Blake Miller has no confirmed budget authority; X10a_Sponsor__c and Who_will_approve_budget__c empty
No Champion: Blake Miller's advocacy and EB/DM access unconfirmed; X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c empty, no Contact Role designation
No quantified pain or metrics: Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c all empty; Next Step mentions initiative but no business case
No decision criteria documented: Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c empty; no RFP/evaluation framework
No competitive intelligence: Competitors__c empty, no mentions in Next Step or calls, cannot position wedge
No decision process mapped: What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c empty; no visibility into approval chain or timeline gates

Single-threaded: only Blake Miller engaged, no other stakeholders identified. If Blake deprioritizes, loses budget, or leaves, deal collapses. August discovery must map EB, DM, and at least 2-3 other influencers (finance, IT, business-unit sponsor).

No Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor identified. For $110K S2P buy, CFO or VP Procurement approval likely required. Without exec alignment, deal cannot progress past discovery. August call must name and begin mapping path to the budget holder.

Zero MEDDPICC depth: no metrics, no pain quantified, no criteria, no process, no champion strength assessed, no competition mapped. Discovery call in early August is the forcing function to extract all of this or walk away. If the call does not deliver substantive answers to Metrics, Pain, EB, Criteria, Process, and Champion, recommend qualify-out.

No buyer calls completed. Zero Avoma transcripts from opp-id or account-id pull (Carmeuse, carmeuse.com domain). All insight to date comes from rep-entered next steps. Buyer voice is absent. August discovery is the first real two-way engagement.

Broad product scope (7 modules: iContract, iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eInvoicing, Merlin, Merlin Intake) for $110K suggests either phased roll-out or small user base. Must clarify deployment model, user count, and whether pricing is realistic or placeholder in August call.
best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage deal with fundamental MEDDPICC and engagement gaps. Zero buyer calls completed, single-threaded (only Blake Miller), no EB/DM/Champion mapped, no pain quantified, no decision process or criteria documented, and no competition identified. Discovery call scheduled for early August 2026 is make-or-break to build foundation or qualify out. Deal is 305 days from close (May 2027), which is realistic for a $110K S2P suite buy IF discovery delivers depth and multi-threading starts immediately.

competitive_position.summary: Competition unknown. No competitors named in Salesforce fields (Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c returned no values), no Avoma calls exist to reveal competitive intel, and no tasks/events reference a rival vendor. Blake Miller is 'in internal discussions with his team' about 'their initiative' (Next_Step__c 2026-06-29), suggesting Carmeuse may still be defining requirements internally and has not yet invited other vendors to the table, or they are running a quiet competitive eval without disclosing it to Zycus. Until the August discovery call, we do not know whether this is sole-source (Zycus only) or multi-vendor (SAP Ariba, Coupa, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua, or another S2P platform). For a $110K S2P suite with Merlin AI, the likely competitive wedge is Merlin's agentic negotiation and intake automation (no other S2P vendor offers this depth in generative AI for supplier-term negotiation) plus unified platform simplicity vs stitched modules, but it cannot be activated until we know who we are being compared against.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning unknown. Merlin and Merlin Intake are in product scope (Salesforce Products__c, authoritative), indicating Zycus has proposed AI-driven intake automation and agentic negotiation as part of the S2P suite, but we do not know whether Carmeuse has AI appetite, agentic-procurement readiness, or how Merlin fits their evaluation criteria. AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) are unpopulated. No Avoma calls exist to assess whether the Merlin story is landing, whether Carmeuse sees AI as a differentiator or a distraction, or whether they have concerns about AI adoption (change management, data readiness, user trust). The August discovery call should probe: what is Carmeuse's AI maturity and appetite, do they see agentic negotiation and intake automation as high-value or experimental, and what would make them bet on Merlin vs a traditional S2P workflow. If AI appetite is low, Merlin may need to be repositioned as optional/Phase 2 rather than core to the initial buy.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Customer expectations unknown. No decision criteria, pain, or business requirements documented in Salesforce or Avoma. Product scope (iContract, iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eInvoicing, Merlin, Merlin Intake) is broad, spanning contracts, sourcing, spend analytics, supplier management, invoicing, and AI-driven intake/negotiation, but we do not know which capabilities Carmeuse values most, what their evaluation criteria are, or whether Merlin AI adoption aligns with their readiness and appetite. The August discovery call must extract what Carmeuse expects from an S2P platform and how Zycus's strengths (unified S2P suite, Merlin agentic AI, ease of use, speed to value) map to those expectations.

hard.ais_why:
• Carmeuse is early: next step is a 'Deeper discovery call setup for first week of August,' with no discovery notes yet captured.
• Merlin and Merlin Intake are scoped, indicating AI-enabled modules are in the frame, but no agentic discussion or sentiment is recorded.
• Evidence is limited to product tags and a scheduling step, so AI appetite cannot be placed above exploratory.

Need to setup a deeper discovery call in a week, the prospect will get back to us about their initiative

Salesforce Q3 custom fields query returned no values for most MEDDPICC and competition fields (likely unpopulated or not present in this org): Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Gaps_identified_during_sales_demo__c, X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Multiple_approvals__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c, Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c, What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Decision_Maker_Name_Title__c, Decision_Maker_Identified__c, Shoe_Fit_Criteria_Met__c, Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Moved_To__c, Why_not_Zycus__c, Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c, Closed_Lost_Reason_Code__c, Current_Contract_Expiration__c. This is a HYGIENE gap (fields not filled), NOT a KNOWLEDGE gap if discovery call delivers the intel.

Salesforce Q2 custom fields query returned no values for AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) and stage-date series (Qualified_Submission_Date__c, Formal_Eval_Submission_Date__c, Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c). Hygiene gap; does not lower confidence if other signals (calls, next steps) provide the insight.

Salesforce OpportunityContactRole query returned only Blake Miller (no title, no role designation, no EB/DM/Champion flag). No other stakeholders. Single-threading is a KNOWLEDGE gap (we genuinely do not know who else is involved) and lowers confidence.

Salesforce field history, tasks, and events queries were not executed in this sweep (not included in tool calls). This is a COVERAGE gap; may have missed competitive intel, commitment history, or stakeholder breadcrumbs in activity records. Recommend including in next sweep if August discovery does not close MEDDPICC gaps.
